name: binwalk
title: binwalk

commands.name: binwalk
commands.description: Tool for searching binary images for embedded files and executable code

arguments.description: Identify the CPU architecture of a file using the capstone disassembler
arguments.raw: -Y, --disasm                 Identify the CPU architecture of a file using the capstone disassembler

arguments.description: Minimum number of consecutive instructions to be considered valid (default: 500)
arguments.raw: -T, --minsn=<int>            Minimum number of consecutive instructions to be considered valid (default: 500)

arguments.description: Don't stop at the first match
arguments.raw: -k, --continue               Don't stop at the first match

arguments.description: Scan target file(s) for common file signatures
arguments.raw: -B, --signature              Scan target file(s) for common file signatures

arguments.description: Scan target file(s) for the specified sequence of bytes
arguments.raw: -R, --raw=<str>              Scan target file(s) for the specified sequence of bytes

arguments.description: Scan target file(s) for common executable opcode signatures
arguments.raw: -A, --opcodes                Scan target file(s) for common executable opcode signatures

arguments.description: Specify a custom magic file to use
arguments.raw: -m, --magic=<file>           Specify a custom magic file to use

arguments.description: Disable smart signature keywords
arguments.raw: -b, --dumb                   Disable smart signature keywords

arguments.description: Show results marked as invalid
arguments.raw: -I, --invalid                Show results marked as invalid

arguments.description: Exclude results that match <str>
arguments.raw: -x, --exclude=<str>          Exclude results that match <str>

arguments.description: Only show results that match <str>
arguments.raw: -y, --include=<str>          Only show results that match <str>

arguments.description: Automatically extract known file types
arguments.raw: -e, --extract                Automatically extract known file types

arguments.description: ext[:cmd]]>  Extract <type> signatures (regular expression), give the files an extension of <ext>, and execute <cmd>
arguments.raw: -D, --dd=<type[:ext[:cmd]]>  Extract <type> signatures (regular expression), give the files an extension of <ext>, and execute <cmd>

arguments.description: Recursively scan extracted files
arguments.raw: -M, --matryoshka             Recursively scan extracted files

arguments.description: Limit matryoshka recursion depth (default: 8 levels deep)
arguments.raw: -d, --depth=<int>            Limit matryoshka recursion depth (default: 8 levels deep)

arguments.description: Extract files/folders to a custom directory (default: current working directory)
arguments.raw: -C, --directory=<str>        Extract files/folders to a custom directory (default: current working directory)

arguments.description: Limit the size of each extracted file
arguments.raw: -j, --size=<int>             Limit the size of each extracted file

arguments.description: Limit the number of extracted files
arguments.raw: -n, --count=<int>            Limit the number of extracted files

arguments.description: Execute external extraction utilities with the specified user's privileges
arguments.raw: -0, --run-as=<str>           Execute external extraction utilities with the specified user's privileges

arguments.description: Do not sanitize extracted symlinks that point outside the extraction directory (dangerous)
arguments.raw: -1, --preserve-symlinks      Do not sanitize extracted symlinks that point outside the extraction directory (dangerous)

arguments.description: Delete carved files after extraction
arguments.raw: -r, --rm                     Delete carved files after extraction

arguments.description: Carve data from files, but don't execute extraction utilities
arguments.raw: -z, --carve                  Carve data from files, but don't execute extraction utilities

arguments.description: Extract into sub-directories named by the offset
arguments.raw: -V, --subdirs                Extract into sub-directories named by the offset

arguments.description: Calculate file entropy
arguments.raw: -E, --entropy                Calculate file entropy

arguments.description: Use faster, but less detailed, entropy analysis
arguments.raw: -F, --fast                   Use faster, but less detailed, entropy analysis

arguments.description: Save plot as a PNG
arguments.raw: -J, --save                   Save plot as a PNG

arguments.description: Omit the legend from the entropy plot graph
arguments.raw: -Q, --nlegend                Omit the legend from the entropy plot graph

arguments.description: Do not generate an entropy plot graph
arguments.raw: -N, --nplot                  Do not generate an entropy plot graph

arguments.description: Set the rising edge entropy trigger threshold (default: 0.95)
arguments.raw: -H, --high=<float>           Set the rising edge entropy trigger threshold (default: 0.95)

arguments.description: Set the falling edge entropy trigger threshold (default: 0.85)
arguments.raw: -L, --low=<float>            Set the falling edge entropy trigger threshold (default: 0.85)

arguments.description: Perform a hexdump / diff of a file or files
arguments.raw: -W, --hexdump                Perform a hexdump / diff of a file or files

arguments.description: Only show lines containing bytes that are the same among all files
arguments.raw: -G, --green                  Only show lines containing bytes that are the same among all files

arguments.description: Only show lines containing bytes that are different among all files
arguments.raw: -i, --red                    Only show lines containing bytes that are different among all files

arguments.description: Only show lines containing bytes that are different among some files
arguments.raw: -U, --blue                   Only show lines containing bytes that are different among some files

arguments.description: Only display lines that are the same between all files
arguments.raw: -u, --similar                Only display lines that are the same between all files

arguments.description: Diff all files, but only display a hex dump of the first file
arguments.raw: -w, --terse                  Diff all files, but only display a hex dump of the first file

arguments.description: Scan for raw deflate compression streams
arguments.raw: -X, --deflate                Scan for raw deflate compression streams

arguments.description: Scan for raw LZMA compression streams
arguments.raw: -Z, --lzma                   Scan for raw LZMA compression streams

arguments.description: Perform a superficial, but faster, scan
arguments.raw: -P, --partial                Perform a superficial, but faster, scan

arguments.description: Stop after the first result
arguments.raw: -S, --stop                   Stop after the first result

arguments.description: Number of bytes to scan
arguments.raw: -l, --length=<int>           Number of bytes to scan

arguments.description: Start scan at this file offset
arguments.raw: -o, --offset=<int>           Start scan at this file offset

arguments.description: Add a base address to all printed offsets
arguments.raw: -O, --base=<int>             Add a base address to all printed offsets

arguments.description: Set file block size
arguments.raw: -K, --block=<int>            Set file block size

arguments.description: Reverse every n bytes before scanning
arguments.raw: -g, --swap=<int>             Reverse every n bytes before scanning

arguments.description: Log results to file
arguments.raw: -f, --log=<file>             Log results to file

arguments.description: Log results to file in CSV format
arguments.raw: -c, --csv                    Log results to file in CSV format

arguments.description: Format output to fit the terminal window
arguments.raw: -t, --term                   Format output to fit the terminal window

arguments.description: Suppress output to stdout
arguments.raw: -q, --quiet                  Suppress output to stdout

arguments.description: Enable verbose output
arguments.raw: -v, --verbose                Enable verbose output

arguments.description: Show help output
arguments.raw: -h, --help                   Show help output

arguments.description: Only scan files whose names match this regex
arguments.raw: -a, --finclude=<str>         Only scan files whose names match this regex

arguments.description: Do not scan files whose names match this regex
arguments.raw: -p, --fexclude=<str>         Do not scan files whose names match this regex

arguments.description: Enable the status server on the specified port
arguments.raw: -s, --status=<int>           Enable the status server on the specified port

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# binwalk -h
 
 Binwalk v2.4.3
 Original author: Craig Heffner, ReFirmLabs
 https://github.com/OSPG/binwalk
 
 Usage: binwalk [OPTIONS] [FILE1] [FILE2] [FILE3] ...
 
 Disassembly Scan Options:
   -Y, --disasm                 Identify the CPU architecture of a file using the capstone disassembler
   -T, --minsn=<int>            Minimum number of consecutive instructions to be considered valid (default: 500)
   -k, --continue               Don't stop at the first match
 
 Signature Scan Options:
   -B, --signature              Scan target file(s) for common file signatures
   -R, --raw=<str>              Scan target file(s) for the specified sequence of bytes
   -A, --opcodes                Scan target file(s) for common executable opcode signatures
   -m, --magic=<file>           Specify a custom magic file to use
   -b, --dumb                   Disable smart signature keywords
   -I, --invalid                Show results marked as invalid
   -x, --exclude=<str>          Exclude results that match <str>
   -y, --include=<str>          Only show results that match <str>
 
 Extraction Options:
   -e, --extract                Automatically extract known file types
   -D, --dd=<type[:ext[:cmd]]>  Extract <type> signatures (regular expression), give the files an extension of <ext>, and execute <cmd>
   -M, --matryoshka             Recursively scan extracted files
   -d, --depth=<int>            Limit matryoshka recursion depth (default: 8 levels deep)
   -C, --directory=<str>        Extract files/folders to a custom directory (default: current working directory)
   -j, --size=<int>             Limit the size of each extracted file
   -n, --count=<int>            Limit the number of extracted files
   -0, --run-as=<str>           Execute external extraction utilities with the specified user's privileges
   -1, --preserve-symlinks      Do not sanitize extracted symlinks that point outside the extraction directory (dangerous)
   -r, --rm                     Delete carved files after extraction
   -z, --carve                  Carve data from files, but don't execute extraction utilities
   -V, --subdirs                Extract into sub-directories named by the offset
 
 Entropy Options:
   -E, --entropy                Calculate file entropy
   -F, --fast                   Use faster, but less detailed, entropy analysis
   -J, --save                   Save plot as a PNG
   -Q, --nlegend                Omit the legend from the entropy plot graph
   -N, --nplot                  Do not generate an entropy plot graph
   -H, --high=<float>           Set the rising edge entropy trigger threshold (default: 0.95)
   -L, --low=<float>            Set the falling edge entropy trigger threshold (default: 0.85)
 
 Binary Diffing Options:
   -W, --hexdump                Perform a hexdump / diff of a file or files
   -G, --green                  Only show lines containing bytes that are the same among all files
   -i, --red                    Only show lines containing bytes that are different among all files
   -U, --blue                   Only show lines containing bytes that are different among some files
   -u, --similar                Only display lines that are the same between all files
   -w, --terse                  Diff all files, but only display a hex dump of the first file
 
 Raw Compression Options:
   -X, --deflate                Scan for raw deflate compression streams
   -Z, --lzma                   Scan for raw LZMA compression streams
   -P, --partial                Perform a superficial, but faster, scan
   -S, --stop                   Stop after the first result
 
 General Options:
   -l, --length=<int>           Number of bytes to scan
   -o, --offset=<int>           Start scan at this file offset
   -O, --base=<int>             Add a base address to all printed offsets
   -K, --block=<int>            Set file block size
   -g, --swap=<int>             Reverse every n bytes before scanning
   -f, --log=<file>             Log results to file
   -c, --csv                    Log results to file in CSV format
   -t, --term                   Format output to fit the terminal window
   -q, --quiet                  Suppress output to stdout
   -v, --verbose                Enable verbose output
   -h, --help                   Show help output
   -a, --finclude=<str>         Only scan files whose names match this regex
   -p, --fexclude=<str>         Do not scan files whose names match this regex
   -s, --status=<int>           Enable the status server on the specified port
 
 [NOTICE] Binwalk v2.x will reach EOL in 12/12/2025. Please migrate to binwalk v3.x

examples.command:
binwalk -B ddwrt-linksys-wrt1200ac-webflash.bin

DECIMAL       HEXADECIMAL     DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
0             0x0             TRX firmware header, little endian, image size: 37883904 bytes, CRC32: 0x95C5DF32, flags: 0x1, version: 1, header size: 28 bytes, loader offset: 0x1C, linux kernel offset: 0x0, rootfs offset: 0x0
28            0x1C            uImage header, header size: 64 bytes, header CRC: 0x780C2742, created: 2018-10-10 02:12:20, image size: 2150281 bytes, Data Address: 0x8000, Entry Point: 0x8000, data CRC: 0xA097CFEA, OS: Linux, CPU: ARM, image type: OS Kernel Image, compression type: none, image name: "DD-WRT"
92            0x5C            Linux kernel ARM boot executable zImage (little-endian)
2460          0x99C           device tree image (dtb)
23432         0x5B88          xz compressed data
23776         0x5CE0          xz compressed data
2117484       0x204F6C        device tree image (dtb)
3145756       0x30001C        UBI erase count header, version: 1, EC: 0x0, VID header offset: 0x800, data offset: 0x1000

local_help.--help:
Binwalk v2.4.3
Original author: Craig Heffner, ReFirmLabs
https://github.com/OSPG/binwalk

Usage: binwalk [OPTIONS] [FILE1] [FILE2] [FILE3] ...

Disassembly Scan Options:
    -Y, --disasm                 Identify the CPU architecture of a file using the capstone disassembler
    -T, --minsn=<int>            Minimum number of consecutive instructions to be considered valid (default: 500)
    -k, --continue               Don't stop at the first match

Signature Scan Options:
    -B, --signature              Scan target file(s) for common file signatures
    -R, --raw=<str>              Scan target file(s) for the specified sequence of bytes
    -A, --opcodes                Scan target file(s) for common executable opcode signatures
    -m, --magic=<file>           Specify a custom magic file to use
    -b, --dumb                   Disable smart signature keywords
    -I, --invalid                Show results marked as invalid
    -x, --exclude=<str>          Exclude results that match <str>
    -y, --include=<str>          Only show results that match <str>

Extraction Options:
    -e, --extract                Automatically extract known file types
    -D, --dd=<type[:ext[:cmd]]>  Extract <type> signatures (regular expression), give the files an extension of <ext>, and execute <cmd>
    -M, --matryoshka             Recursively scan extracted files
    -d, --depth=<int>            Limit matryoshka recursion depth (default: 8 levels deep)
    -C, --directory=<str>        Extract files/folders to a custom directory (default: current working directory)
    -j, --size=<int>             Limit the size of each extracted file
    -n, --count=<int>            Limit the number of extracted files
    -0, --run-as=<str>           Execute external extraction utilities with the specified user's privileges
    -1, --preserve-symlinks      Do not sanitize extracted symlinks that point outside the extraction directory (dangerous)
    -r, --rm                     Delete carved files after extraction
    -z, --carve                  Carve data from files, but don't execute extraction utilities
    -V, --subdirs                Extract into sub-directories named by the offset

Entropy Options:
    -E, --entropy                Calculate file entropy
    -F, --fast                   Use faster, but less detailed, entropy analysis
    -J, --save                   Save plot as a PNG
    -Q, --nlegend                Omit the legend from the entropy plot graph
    -N, --nplot                  Do not generate an entropy plot graph
    -H, --high=<float>           Set the rising edge entropy trigger threshold (default: 0.95)
    -L, --low=<float>            Set the falling edge entropy trigger threshold (default: 0.85)

Binary Diffing Options:
    -W, --hexdump                Perform a hexdump / diff of a file or files
    -G, --green                  Only show lines containing bytes that are the same among all files
    -i, --red                    Only show lines containing bytes that are different among all files
    -U, --blue                   Only show lines containing bytes that are different among some files
    -u, --similar                Only display lines that are the same between all files
    -w, --terse                  Diff all files, but only display a hex dump of the first file

Raw Compression Options:
    -X, --deflate                Scan for raw deflate compression streams
    -Z, --lzma                   Scan for raw LZMA compression streams
    -P, --partial                Perform a superficial, but faster, scan
    -S, --stop                   Stop after the first result

General Options:
    -l, --length=<int>           Number of bytes to scan
    -o, --offset=<int>           Start scan at this file offset
    -O, --base=<int>             Add a base address to all printed offsets
    -K, --block=<int>            Set file block size
    -g, --swap=<int>             Reverse every n bytes before scanning
    -f, --log=<file>             Log results to file
    -c, --csv                    Log results to file in CSV format
    -t, --term                   Format output to fit the terminal window
    -q, --quiet                  Suppress output to stdout
    -v, --verbose                Enable verbose output
    -h, --help                   Show help output
    -a, --finclude=<str>         Only scan files whose names match this regex
    -p, --fexclude=<str>         Do not scan files whose names match this regex
    -s, --status=<int>           Enable the status server on the specified port

[NOTICE] Binwalk v2.x will reach EOL in 12/12/2025. Please migrate to binwalk v3.x

local_help.man:
BINWALK(1)                       User Commands                       BINWALK(1)

NAME
     binwalk  -  tool  for  searching binary images for embedded files and exe-
     cutable code

SYNOPSIS
     binwalk [OPTIONS] [FILE1] [FILE2] [FILE3] ...

DESCRIPTION
     Binwalk v2.3.2+dcb1403 Craig  Heffner,  ReFirmLabs  https://github.com/Re-
     FirmLabs/binwalk

   Signature Scan Options:
     -B, --signature
            Scan target file(s) for common file signatures

     -R, --raw=<str>
            Scan target file(s) for the specified sequence of bytes

     -A, --opcodes
            Scan target file(s) for common executable opcode signatures

     -m, --magic=<file>
            Specify a custom magic file to use

     -b, --dumb
            Disable smart signature keywords

     -I, --invalid
            Show results marked as invalid

     -x, --exclude=<str>
            Exclude results that match <str>

     -y, --include=<str>
            Only show results that match <str>

   Extraction Options:
     -e, --extract
            Automatically extract known file types

     -D, --dd=<type[:ext[:cmd]]>
            Extract  <type>  signatures (regular expression), give the files an
            extension of <ext>, and execute <cmd>

     -M, --matryoshka
            Recursively scan extracted files

     -d, --depth=<int>
            Limit matryoshka recursion depth (default: 8 levels deep)

     -C, --directory=<str>
            Extract files/folders to a custom directory (default: current work-
            ing directory)

     -j, --size=<int>
            Limit the size of each extracted file

     -n, --count=<int>
            Limit the number of extracted files

     -r, --rm
            Delete carved files after extraction

     -z, --carve
            Carve data from files, but don't execute extraction utilities

     -V, --subdirs
            Extract into sub-directories named by the offset

   Entropy Options:
     -E, --entropy
            Calculate file entropy

     -F, --fast
            Use faster, but less detailed, entropy analysis

     -J, --save
            Save plot as a PNG

     -Q, --nlegend
            Omit the legend from the entropy plot graph

     -N, --nplot
            Do not generate an entropy plot graph

     -H, --high=<float>
            Set the rising edge entropy trigger threshold (default: 0.95)

     -L, --low=<float>
            Set the falling edge entropy trigger threshold (default: 0.85)

   Binary Diffing Options:
     -W, --hexdump
            Perform a hexdump / diff of a file or files

     -G, --green
            Only show lines containing bytes that are the same among all files

     -i, --red
            Only show lines containing bytes that are different among all files

     -U, --blue
            Only show lines containing bytes  that  are  different  among  some
            files

     -u, --similar
            Only display lines that are the same between all files

     -w, --terse
            Diff all files, but only display a hex dump of the first file

   Raw Compression Options:
     -X, --deflate
            Scan for raw deflate compression streams

     -Z, --lzma
            Scan for raw LZMA compression streams

     -P, --partial
            Perform a superficial, but faster, scan

     -S, --stop
            Stop after the first result

   General Options:
     -l, --length=<int>
            Number of bytes to scan

     -o, --offset=<int>
            Start scan at this file offset

     -O, --base=<int>
            Add a base address to all printed offsets

     -K, --block=<int>
            Set file block size

     -g, --swap=<int>
            Reverse every n bytes before scanning

     -f, --log=<file>
            Log results to file

     -c, --csv
            Log results to file in CSV format

     -t, --term
            Format output to fit the terminal window

     -q, --quiet
            Suppress output to stdout

     -v, --verbose
            Enable verbose output

     -h, --help
            Show help output

     -a, --finclude=<str>
            Only scan files whose names match this regex

     -p, --fexclude=<str>
            Do not scan files whose names match this regex

     -s, --status=<int>
            Enable the status server on the specified port

local_help.apropos: binwalk (1)          - tool for searching binary images for embedded files an...

apt_metadata.description:
tool library for analyzing binary blobs and executable code
Binwalk is a tool for searching a given binary image for embedded files
and executable code. Specifically, it is designed for identifying files
and code embedded inside of firmware images. Binwalk uses the libmagic
library, so it is compatible with magic signatures created for the Unix
file utility.
.
Binwalk also includes a custom magic signature file which contains
improved signatures for files that are commonly found in firmware images
such as compressed/archived files, firmware headers, Linux kernels,
bootloaders, filesystems, etc.
.
This package is an empty package, because the binary tool is already
provided with the library, dependency of this package.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Pair with the LLM system-prompt stanza to ensure the chain planner knows how to call this tool.
